ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for chocolate frosted donut; SNOMED CT coverage for commercial baked/fried confectionery is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard chocolate frosted donut weighing approximately 60 g, as typically sold at donut chains in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Doughnuts, cake-type, chocolate-coated or frosted (SR Legacy NDB No. 18249)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cake-type chocolate-frosted doughnut (NDB No. 18249) used as the primary nutrient anchor. This is an approved secondary global source under Project Veritas v2 source policy. Per-100g values from the input record (GrabFood AI-generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against USDA SR Legacy data for this food category and found broadly consistent. Energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron, cholesterol, saturated fat, and trans fat values retained from input where consistent with USDA ranges. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1 as detailed fatty acid breakdown is not separately reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for this specific product variant.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Doughnut, cake type (GI=76, glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 76 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database as published in Atkinson, Foster-Powell and Brand-Miller (Diabetes Care, 2008) for cake-type doughnut (glucose reference scale). This is an approved GI-specific source under Project Veritas v2 source policy. The chocolate frosted variant is not separately listed in the GI database; cake-type doughnut is the closest defensible analogue given the identical deep-fried refined-flour base. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (76 × 27.0) / 100 = 20.5, where available carbohydrate per 60g serving = 45.0 × 0.60 = 27.0g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (30g/100g), high saturated fat (11.7g/100g), and moderate sodium (300mg/100g) with minimal fibre offset
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid this food entirely: the combination of 30g/100g sugar, 11.7g/100g saturated fat, and 300mg/100g sodium with only 2.5g/100g dietary fibre promotes arterial stiffness, weight gain, and blood pressure elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sugar (30g/100g) and high GI (76) with low fibre (2.5g/100g) causing rapid, large postprandial glucose spike
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes must avoid this food: sugar at 30g/100g far exceeds the 20g/100g glycaemic ceiling, the GI of 76 is firmly in the high range, and the glycaemic load per standard 60g serving is 20.5 — all of which produce an unacceptable postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (11.7g/100g), trans fat (0.5g/100g), and high sugar (30g/100g) in a deep-fried ultra-processed product
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid this food: saturated fat at 11.7g/100g and trans fat at 0.5g/100g raise LDL cholesterol and promote atherogenesis, while the high sugar load and deep-fried preparation further increase cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation generating advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and high saturated fat (11.7g/100g) driving pro-inflammatory cytokines
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this food entirely: deep-frying generates AGEs that worsen joint inflammation, and saturated fat at 11.7g/100g promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 production, while sugar at 30g/100g further amplifies inflammatory mediators.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chocolate Frosted Donut (also sold as 'Choc Frost') is a deep-fried, ring-shaped confection made from an enriched wheat-flour dough that is leavened with yeast or chemical leaveners, deep-fried in palm or vegetable oil until golden, and then coated with a rich dark chocolate glaze or frosting. The result is a soft, pillowy interior with a slightly crisp exterior and a thick, sweet chocolate shell. The flavour profile is intensely sweet with a pronounced cocoa bitterness from the dark chocolate frosting. Per 100g, this product delivers approximately 416 kcal, 5.8g protein, 23.3g fat (of which 11.7g is saturated fat and 0.5g is trans fat), 45g carbohydrate, and 30g sugar, with only 2.5g dietary fibre and 300mg sodium. The glycaemic index is approximately 76 (high), producing a glycaemic load of about 20.5 per standard 60g serving. Clinically, this food is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its very high sugar content, high saturated and trans fat levels, high GI, and pro-inflammatory deep-fried preparation. It is classified as an ultra-processed food. In Singapore, it is commonly sold at donut chain outlets and coffee shops as a snack or breakfast item. It is halal-certified and vegetarian-suitable but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for cake-type chocolate-frosted doughnut (NDB No. 18249), which is an approved secondary global source and the closest available analogue. The input record values (GrabFood AI-generated, confidence 0.7) are broadly consistent with USDA SR Legacy ranges for this food category. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) for cake-type doughnut. Detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA, PUFA), potassium, and most micronutrient data are not available for this specific product variant in any approved source, hence medium rather than high confidence.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for chocolate frosted donut not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific commercial chocolate frosted donut variant not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cake-type chocolate-frosted doughnut (NDB No. 18249, approved secondary global source); input GrabFood AI-generated values were cross-checked and found broadly consistent with USDA ranges. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database as published in Atkinson, Foster-Powell and Brand-Miller (Diabetes Care, 2008) for cake-type doughnut; chocolate frosted variant is not separately listed. Glycaemic load per 60g serving = (76 × 27.0) / 100 = 20.5. This food is sold in Singapore under the brand name 'Choc Frost' and is classified as halal and vegetarian. No zero-value core macros are present in this record. All aliases_by_language entries verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 18249, approved secondary global source) for cake-type chocolate-frosted doughnut, consistent with input values. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database as published in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care, approved GI-specific source). All mandatory Singaporean-dish multilingual names are present. Ontology anchors (FoodOn and FoodEx2) are defensible and not invented. All three required health conditions are present with specific clinical guidance and correct human_condition_name for Type 2 Diabetes. Flat aliases array is fully consistent with aliases_by_language. The food is correctly classified as ultra-processed, high-GI, and unsuitable for diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis.
